spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the per-frame consumer side of the API — read `frame.rotation` and `frame.flip` and apply the equivalent canvas transform before `ctx.drawImage(frame)`. That is exactly the consumer recipe the spec adds these accessors for.

interactive.rationale: Select for source rotation + button to redraw, with 3 listeners driving a live side-by-side: naive vs orientation-aware drawImage. Real interactive comparison, not a code card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises `side-by-side comparison: naive ctx.drawImage(frame) that ignores orientation metadata vs the correct approach that reads frame.rotation and frame.flip and applies the canvas transform first`. The page delivers both panels.

edge_cases.rationale: Naive-vs-correct covers the main failure; no explicit demo of the case where `rotation === 0` and `flip === false` (no transform applied — the cheap path) nor the order-of-operations question (flip then rotate vs rotate then flip).

references.rationale: Page has no `see also`/`references` section at all — sections list is empty. Missing the chromestatus.com/feature/5128855343595520 link required by invariant #3.

design_system.rationale: Inline styles include raw hex (`#f8f8f0`, `#2563eb`, `#e11d48`, `#059669`, `#b45309`) and bypass the variable palette in `public/styles.css`. Invariant #5 only permits the defined variables.

openQuestions.title: Add a references section with the chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: The page has no `references`/`see also` section. Invariant #3 requires every concept page to cite `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5128855343595520` so the slug-recovery cleanup script can self-heal. Add the section with chromestatus + WebCodecs spec link.

openQuestions.title: Replace raw hex palette with design-system variables
openQuestions.detail: Inline styles use `#f8f8f0`, `#2563eb`, `#e11d48`, `#059669`, `#b45309` — none are in the design system. Map each to its closest variable from `public/styles.css` (likely `--bg-ivory`, `--accent-blue`, `--accent-rose`, `--accent-emerald`, etc).

openQuestions.title: Show the `no transform` zero-cost branch
openQuestions.detail: When `rotation === 0` and `flip === false`, the orientation-aware code path should skip the transform entirely (cheap). Add a third panel or annotation that demonstrates this branch — readers should see that the orientation-aware path is not a perf tax for the common case.

openQuestions.title: Clarify flip-then-rotate vs rotate-then-flip order
openQuestions.detail: Whether `flip` is applied before or after `rotation` matters when both are set. Either link out to the spec text that defines the order, or add a small explanatory block showing the same input rendered with both orderings so the reader knows which one matches what the browser does.

summary: The right shape — a real naive-vs-correct drawImage comparison driven by the new `frame.rotation`/`frame.flip` accessors — but it ships missing the references section entirely and using a non-system hex palette. Both are mechanical fixes; the underlying demo is on the spec.
